description: Quantifies the agreement between the unsupervised Leiden partition and the Mathlib top-level MODULE labels over all 84465 module-labelled nodes: NMI, AMI, homogeneity, completeness, V-measure, ARI (sklearn). Plus structural diagnostics of what clustering ADDS: per-module fragmentation (# Leiden clusters a module spans, fraction of module in its largest cluster) and per-cluster mixing (# modules a big cluster merges, dominant-module fraction).
motivation: q-0022 (reviewer EvB8 / meta-review): if domains are already recoverable from module labels, does unsupervised clustering merely confirm the labels (no contribution) or carve the space differently?
results_description: 84465 labelled nodes, 137 Leiden clusters vs 30 modules. NMI=0.303, AMI=0.300, V-measure=0.303, ARI=0.139 -- only ~30% shared information, far from re-derivation (NMI~1). Homogeneity (cluster=one module)=0.310 ~ Completeness (module=one cluster)=0.297: roughly symmetric divergence. Fragmentation: each module spans on average 22.4 Leiden clusters; MEDIAN module has only 44.1% of its nodes in its single largest cluster (Data spans 70 clusters / 22% in largest, Algebra 54 / 22%, Analysis 47 / 32%). Mixing: the 24 big clusters (>=200 nodes) merge 24-27 modules each; 18/24 have dominant-module fraction <0.50.
conclusions: Unsupervised Leiden clustering does NOT merely re-derive the module labels: node-level agreement is MODEST (NMI 0.30, ARI 0.14), and the partitions diverge symmetrically (homogeneity ~ completeness ~ 0.30). Clustering carves a substantially DIFFERENT, dependency-driven partition -- it both fragments modules (avg 22 clusters/module, median 44% in largest) and merges them (big clusters span 24-27 modules). So the methodological contribution is real (it yields structure not present in the labels), BUT this cuts against the project's own 'clustering recovers named domains' headline (a-0001): recovery is partial -- clusters are NAMED by their plurality module (often ~30% dominance), which is weaker than node-level domain recovery. Net: the value of clustering is the dependency-driven re-carving, not label confirmation; and the 'recovers domains' claim should be read as 'clusters are nameable by a plurality module', not 'reproduces the module partition'.
